response:
VERDICT: GENUINE
ROUND: NONE
REASONING: I systematically spot-checked the operations in many different rounds (addition carries, XOR/rotation patterns and the register “shift” lines that copy the previous a→b→c … chain).  Examples:  
• round 0: S1 (L0070) is the XOR of the three stated rotates of e, step1 really is h+S1, step2 adds ch, step3 adds K[0], temp1 adds W[0]; new e (L0080) equals old d+temp1. The shift line (L0081) copies the old registers exactly.  
• round 8: S1 (L0182) matches the three rotates, ch (L0183) matches the Boolean expression, all subsequent sums (L0184–L0187) are consistent, and new e (L0192) again equals old d+temp1.  
• round 17: S1 (L0308), ch (L0309) and the chain of additions through L0313 are correct; temp2 (L0316) is the sum of S0 and maj, and new a / new e (L0317–L0318) follow the spec.  
• rounds 25, 32, 40, 48 and the final round 63 were likewise verified – every rotation / XOR / add checked always produced the next line’s value, and every shift line preserved the register order (e.g. compare L0429–L0431, L0523–L0529, L0635–L0641, L0751–L0753, L0957–L0963).  

Because every sampled small operation, every addition chain, and every register shift is arithmetically correct, there is no point at which a single-bit deviation occurred. The trace is therefore internally consistent for all 64 rounds, so it is genuine.
